sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.81 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Does there exist a class$$of finite groups satisfying the following conditions: [r]{(1)}$$is closed with respect to taking subgroups and homomorphic images; [r]{(2)} the product of two normal$$-subgroups of an arbitrary group is always an$$-group; [r]{(3)} for every positive integer$n$, there exist a finite group and its conjugacy class$D$such that any$n$elements of$D$generate an$$-subgroup, where...
